design_philosophy.core_loop: Accept missions, fly fighter/freighter craft through space combat scenarios, return to base. Manage energy distribution between weapons, shields, and engines. Command multi-crew vessels or fly solo fighters.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Dual loyalty: serving Rebel Alliance military objectives while protecting family business interests amid a family feud with the Viraxo. Personal stakes (family members killed early) drive narrative tension alongside large-scale galactic conflict.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Be a skilled Star Wars pilot, progressing from freighter pilot to elite Rebel fighter ace. Command the Millennium Falcon. Participate in iconic movie moments (Battle of Endor) while building personal legacy through family storyline.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Integrates personal narrative (family drama, mail system, room souvenirs) into military simulation framework. Unlike pure tactical sims, emphasizes character relationships and emotional investment. Multi-crew ship control allows role flexibility. Simulation respects player skill in energy management and positioning rather than arcade reflex.

difficulty.continues_system: Three mission skips allow players to bypass difficult missions and continue linear story progression. Family missions cannot be skipped, creating mandatory difficulty spikes.
difficulty.checkpointing: Mid-mission checkpoints via hangar bay stops; players can rearm and repair mid-engagement without restarting entire mission.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Mission skip system provides safety valve for casual players unwilling to master difficult encounters. Flexible multi-crew controls allow players to manage workload (control turret vs. pilot duties). Energy management system rewards learned skill.

progression.upgrade_system: Craft roster expansion through mission availability. Access to larger, more heavily-armed freighters (Corellian transports, Millennium Falcon) unlocked via story progression. Energy linking system allows upgraded weapon configuration.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-driven mission progression. Family missions unlock new craft. Iconic vessels like Millennium Falcon become available during narrative appropriate moments. Custom mission builder unlocks after campaign completion with all encountered vessels available.
replayability_hooks: Custom mission builder with dozens of flyable fighters and combat transports
Multiplayer modes allowing PvP and cooperative engagement scenarios
Collectible souvenirs in Ace's room providing completionist hook
Multiple ship roles (pilot vs. turret operator in multi-crew vessels) enable different playstyles

world_building_techniques: Full voice-over dialogue during flights and missions
Mail system between missions revealing character relationships and family dynamics
Environmental storytelling via souvenir collection in Ace's room serving as visual memoir
Story missions connected to Star Wars movie timeline (Battle of Hoth, Battle of Endor, Shadows of the Empire crossover)
Character-driven narrative: family members introduced, killed (father and brother), driving personal motivation
In-flight dialogue providing real-time tactical context and relationship development

faction_indicators: Rebel Alliance membership as primary faction (progresses through military ranks)
Azzameen family loyalty and business reputation (competes against Viraxo family enterprise)
Imperial forces as persistent antagonist faction
danger_signals: Imperial raid on family station; family casualties create permanent loss mechanic
Discovery of Imperial experimental weapons and Death Star projects raise existential stakes
environmental_storytelling: Family home station destruction visualizes stakes of galactic conflict at personal scale
Battlefield expansion from early small skirmishes to 96-ship engagements demonstrates growing war scale
Iconic locations (Endor, second Death Star) ground fictional narrative in movie canon
